case_title: CAI XIAO SHENG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

NTC OF NON-OPPOS. TO MTN TO STRIKE TO PLTF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ON THE ISSUE OF STANDING AS TO PLTF KAYLEE J CHOY FILED BY DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER
DECLARATION OF CAI XIAO SHENG FILED BY PLAINTIFF SHENG, CAI XIAO CHOY, KAYLEE J.
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-27-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOS TO DEFTS DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF SHENG, CAI XIAO CHOY, KAYLEE J.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CAI XIAO SHENG IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SHOWING THE EXISTENCE OF MEDICAL EXPERTISE OR TRAINING THAT GUIDED HIS UNDERSTANDING OF HIS WIFE'S MEDICAL TREATMENT. PLAINTIFF KAYLEE J. CHOY LACKS CAPACITY TO SUE IN HER OWN NAME. PLAINTIFF CAI XIAO SHENG IS APPOINTED AS HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM, BUT MUST HIRE COUNSEL TO REPRESENT HIM AND HIS DAUGHTER IN THAT CAPACITY. IF COUNSEL IS NOT SUBSTITUTED IN THE CASE WITHIN THIRTY DAYS, DEFENDANT MAY, PER EX PARTE APPLICATION, SEEK THE DISMISSAL OF CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF CAI XIAO SHENG AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR KAYLEE J. CHOY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-20-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 20, 2012 PER THE REQUEST IN DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION TO ENABLE THE PARTIES TO ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF THE STANDING OF PLAINTIFF KAYLEE CHOY. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION WILL BE TREATED AS AN AMENDMENT TO ITS DEMURRER AS TO THIS ISSUE AND PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE OPPOSITION PAPERS ON ALL ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER, INCLUDING THE STANDING OF KAYLEE CHOY, BY MARCH 8 AND DEFENDANT IS GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE FURTHER REPLY PAPERS BY MARCH 14. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-27-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS ARE STRICKEN. IF PLAINTIFF CAI XIAO SHENG WISHES TO SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES, HE NEEDS TO FOLLOW THE PROCEDURE SET FORTH IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 425.13. PLAINTIFF KAYLEE J. CHOY LACKS CAPACITY TO SUE IN HER OWN NAME. PLAINTIFF CAI XIAO SHENG IS APPOINTED AS HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM, BUT MUST HIRE COUNSEL TO REPRESENT HIM AND HIS DAUGHTER IN THAT CAPACITY. IF COUNSEL IS NOT SUBSTITUTED IN THE CASE WITHIN THIRTY DAYS, DEFENDANT MAY, PER EX PARTE APPLICATION, SEEK THE DISMISSAL OF CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF CAI XIAO SHENG AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR KAYLEE J. CHOY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marcell Patrick Neri, Esq., of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Granted. The punitive damages allegations are stricken. If Plaintiff Cai Xiao Sheng wishes to seek punitive damages, he needs to follow the procedure set forth in Code of Civil Procedure 425.13. Plaintiff Kaylee J. Choy lacks capacity to sue in her own name. Plaintiff Cai Xiao Sheng is appointed as her guardian ad litem, but must hire counsel to represent him and his daughter in that capacity. If counsel is not substituted in the case within thirty days, defendant may, per ex parte application, seek the dismissal of claims alleged by plaintiff Cai Xiao Sheng as guardian ad litem for Kaylee J. Choy. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marcell Patrick Neri, Esq., of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained as to the third cause of action with thirty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Cai Xiao Sheng is given leave to amend to allege, if he can do so in good faith, facts showing the existence of medical expertise or training that guided his understanding of his wife's medical treatment. Plaintiff Kaylee J. Choy lacks capacity to sue in her own name. Plaintiff Cai Xiao Sheng is appointed as her guardian ad litem, but must hire counsel to represent him and his daughter in that capacity. If counsel is not substituted in the case within thirty days, defendant may, per ex parte application, seek the dismissal of claims alleged by plaintiff Cai Xiao Sheng as guardian ad litem for Kaylee J. Choy. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2012 Continued To Mar-20-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Continued to March 20, 2012 per the request in defendant's notice of non-opposition to enable the parties to address the issue of the standing of plaintiff Kaylee Choy. Defendant's notice of non-opposition will be treated as an amendment to its demurrer as to this issue and plaintiffs are given leave to file opposition papers on all issues raised by the demurrer, including the standing of Kaylee Choy, by March 8 and defendant is given leave to file further reply papers by March 14. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353
